prompt:
Passage 1
A regional manufacturing plant recently changed how it handles tool calibration reminders. Previously, supervisors checked a spreadsheet at the end of each month to see which tools needed calibration. This process sometimes allowed tools to remain in use past their recommended calibration date, especially if the spreadsheet had not been updated promptly.
The plant now uses a tracking system that sends automatic reminders before a tool is due for calibration. Each tool record includes the tool identification number, department, last calibration date, next due date, and current status. Employees can check whether a tool is available, due soon, or temporarily removed from use.
Managers report that the new system has made calibration schedules easier to follow. It also helps prevent tools from being used when their status is uncertain.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Supervisors stopped checking tool records.
choices.B: Automatic reminders improved the plant’s tool calibration process.
choices.C: Employees now calibrate tools without records.
choices.D: The plant removed all tools from use.

explanation: The passage explains how automatic reminders made calibration schedules easier to follow and helped prevent use of tools with uncertain status.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning March 18, employees who use the shared maintenance cart must return all supplies to the labeled drawers before the cart is placed back in storage. The cart should also be checked for missing items at the end of each shift.
This procedure was created after several employees reported that common supplies, such as tape, markers, small fasteners, and gloves, were missing when the cart was needed. In some cases, workers had to leave the job area to search for replacement supplies.
The goal is to keep the cart ready for the next user and reduce time lost looking for basic materials.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To explain a procedure for keeping the shared maintenance cart organized
choices.B: To remove the maintenance cart from service permanently
choices.C: To announce a new employee break schedule
choices.D: To require customers to restock supplies

explanation: The notice explains how employees should return supplies and check the shared maintenance cart so it stays ready for use.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many organizations use written emergency contact lists to prepare for unexpected events. These lists may include supervisors, facility managers, security contacts, utility providers, equipment vendors, and local emergency services.
A contact list is useful only if the information is accurate. An outdated phone number can delay a response when time matters. For this reason, many companies review emergency contact lists on a regular schedule and after major staffing changes.
The list should also be easy to find. If employees must search through old emails or several folders during an emergency, the list may not serve its purpose. Some workplaces post printed copies in key areas and store digital copies in shared locations.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Emergency contact lists are useful only when they are current and easy to find.
choices.B: Employees should search old emails during emergencies.
choices.C: Emergency contact lists should include only customers.
choices.D: Companies should avoid updating phone numbers.

explanation: The passage explains that emergency contact lists are useful only when the information is accurate and easy to access.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before automated teller machines became common, many banking tasks required customers to visit a bank branch during business hours. Customers who needed cash after hours often had few convenient options.
ATMs changed banking by allowing customers to withdraw cash, check balances, and complete certain transactions without speaking to a teller. This made basic banking services available at more times and in more locations. Banks also benefited because some routine transactions could be handled without waiting in line inside the branch.
ATMs did not replace all banking services. Customers may still need bank employees for loans, account problems, or services that require personal review. However, ATMs made routine access to money and account information more convenient.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: ATMs made routine banking services more convenient.
choices.B: Bank branches stopped offering all services.
choices.C: Customers can use ATMs only during business hours.
choices.D: ATMs replaced every bank employee.

explanation: The passage focuses on how ATMs made routine banking services more convenient and available outside traditional branch hours.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional shipping company recently added a weather review step to its daily route planning. Dispatchers now check weather conditions before assigning long-distance deliveries. The review includes forecasts for heavy rain, snow, high winds, and road closures.
Before the change, drivers sometimes learned about severe weather only after starting their route. This could lead to delays, route changes, or unsafe driving conditions. The new review does not eliminate weather problems, but it gives dispatchers and drivers more time to plan.
When poor weather is expected, dispatchers may adjust departure times, choose alternate routes, or contact customers about possible delays. Managers say the goal is to make safer and more realistic delivery plans.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company stopped making long-distance deliveries.
choices.B: A weather review step helps the company plan routes more safely and realistically.
choices.D: Customers are responsible for checking weather forecasts.

explanation: The passage explains how weather reviews help dispatchers and drivers plan safer and more realistic delivery routes.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many workplaces use shared digital calendars to coordinate training sessions, inspections, deadlines, and staff meetings. These calendars allow employees to see upcoming events without waiting for individual reminders.
A shared calendar can reduce confusion when multiple departments are involved. For example, if a safety inspection requires both maintenance and operations employees to be present, placing the event on a shared calendar helps both groups prepare.
However, shared calendars must be maintained. If an event is canceled but remains on the calendar, employees may waste time preparing for something that will not happen. If an event is added too late, employees may not have enough time to adjust their schedules.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Shared digital calendars can improve coordination when they are kept accurate.
choices.B: Employees should avoid checking upcoming events.
choices.C: Calendars are useful only for personal appointments.
choices.D: Canceled events should remain on shared calendars.

explanation: The passage explains that shared digital calendars improve coordination only when event information is maintained accurately.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities use public service announcements to share important information with residents. These announcements may explain changes to trash pickup, water service interruptions, road closures, public health clinics, or emergency procedures.
A good announcement is clear, brief, and specific. Residents need to know what is happening, who is affected, when the change occurs, and what action they should take. If an announcement gives too much background but leaves out the required action, residents may still be confused.
Public service announcements may be shared through websites, text alerts, flyers, radio messages, or social media. Using several channels can help reach residents who get information in different ways.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Public service announcements help residents when they are clear and specific.
choices.B: Residents should avoid public information.
choices.C: Public announcements should include as little useful information as possible.
choices.D: Communities use announcements only for entertainment.

explanation: The passage explains that public service announcements help residents when they clearly explain what is happening and what action to take.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation class recently added short confidence checks before practice exams. Students are asked to rate how prepared they feel in reading, math, and test pacing. They also write one concern they want to focus on during the practice exam.
Instructors added the activity after noticing that some students could not clearly describe what made them nervous. One student might say “I am bad at tests,” when the real issue was running out of time on reading passages. Another might feel worried about math but actually struggle most with setting up word problems.
The confidence checks help instructors provide more focused advice. They also help students turn general worry into a specific study target.

Why did the class add confidence checks?

choices.A: Some students could not clearly describe what made them nervous.
choices.B: Students stopped taking practice exams.
choices.C: Instructors wanted to remove reading and math practice.
choices.D: The class no longer worked on test pacing.

explanation: The confidence checks were added because some students could not clearly describe what made them nervous.

choices.D: Finishing every section early

explanation: The passage gives the example that a student who says “I am bad at tests” may actually be running out of time on reading passages.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation class recently added short confidence checks before practice exams. Students are asked to rate how prepared they feel in reading, math, and test pacing. They also write one concern they want to focus on during the practice exam.
Instructors added the activity after noticing that some students could not clearly describe what made them nervous. One student might say “I am bad at tests,” when the real issue was running out of time on reading passages. Another might feel worried about math but actually struggle most with setting up word problems.
The confidence checks help instructors provide more focused advice. They also help students turn general worry into a specific study target.

Which statement is best supported by the passage?

choices.A: Identifying a specific concern can make studying more focused.
choices.B: General worry is always more useful than a specific study target.
choices.C: Confidence checks prevent instructors from giving advice.
choices.D: Students should avoid thinking about preparation before practice exams.

explanation: The passage supports the idea that turning general worry into a specific concern helps create a more focused study target.
